GateDocument.TextWithNodes: "This cult Australian furniture brand just released a brand new sofa", "And it's vegan!", "There's something about the feeling of sitting on a fresh new piece of furniture. Similar to the buyer's high at the point of purchase, the moment you sit down on your new sofa... well, it's seriously satisfying. And as everyone's favourite online mattress brand, Koala.com, continues to expand its furniture range, the process of initial elation to that first sit is remarkably fast. Four hours, to be exact. Releasing what is no doubt its most stylish piece to date, Koala.com has totaled its living room furniture range to four pieces altogether, with a 2.0 version of its beautifully designed sofa guaranteed to work for every home. Available in two and three seaters, plus an ottoman style and in two colours (a brushtail grey and a blue), the couch is uber comfortable and super versatile – and, like all Koala furniture, can be put together with no tools. Yep, we're sold. Made with sturdy yet soft fabric and built from sustainably-sourced wood, the sofa is vegan and will prove to stand the test of time. "We have continued with a textured two toned yarn to give surface interest. The slight strike effect elevates the fabric, which gives it the perfect look for every interior," Koala product designer Josh Morabito explains, with fellow designer Adam Harrigan adding what the team intentionally left out. "No trendy short term colours, no funky curves, no unnecessary features, it needed to have longevity and be simple and easy to adapt to any interior style." Plus, it works perfectly in just about every room, from tiny inner-city apartments to suburban living rooms. "As Koala's stylist I've assembled their sofas and bookshelves a million times, everything they design has been so well thought out that you could basically put it together with your eyes closed it's so easy," interior stylist Tahnee Carroll says. Stylist and interiors expert Jono Fleming agrees. "The Koala Sofa is where practicality meets comfort and affordability," he confirms. Paired best with simple accessories, like a low side table and a neutral rug, add a pop of natural colour with an indoor plant – our pick is a tall palm or a classic fiddle leaf fig. The colours are subdued and classic. For the room that needs a pop of colour, choose the blue option and pair with creams, forest greens and ochres. Neutral lovers should opt for the brushtail dove grey and pair with like-colours – think whites and browns – for an on-trend minimalist look. Visit: Koala.com"
